close_reason: Implemented: removed daily/sessions tables, added linked stat cards (a.stat-card), 2 new cards (Avg Messages/Session, Reasoning Tokens), 3 quick-glance sections (Recent Sessions, Top Projects, Top Models). rootHandler fetches in parallel. All 148 tests pass.
description:
Final piece — refactor src/ui/templates/dashboard.ts and rootHandler. Blocked by ALL four detail pages being done (so links are valid destinations).

DECISIONS LOCKED:
- Daily breakdown table: REMOVED from front page (follow dashboard-detail-pages.md). Rationale: avoids duplicate data surface; a dedicated /daily index page can be added later if needed without touching the dashboard.
- Sessions template filename: sessions-list.ts (see p404-fky).

Changes to src/ui/templates/dashboard.ts:
1. REMOVE 30-row daily breakdown table (was: dailyList())
2. REMOVE 50-row sessions table (was: sessionsTable())
3. ADD 2 new overview cards: Avg Messages/Session (avgMessagesPerSession) and Reasoning Tokens (totalTokensReasoning)
4. WRAP linkable stat cards in <a> tags:
   - Sessions → /sessions
   - Messages → /sessions
   - Total Cost → /sessions
   - Avg Cost/Session → /sessions
   - Input Tokens → /models
   - Output Tokens → /models
   CSS class: a.stat-card (added in p404-6c2)
5. ADD 3 quick-glance sections (5 rows each, .quick-section + 'View all →' link):
   - Recent Sessions (top 5 by time_updated DESC) → /sessions
     Columns: Project · Title · Cost · Date
   - Top Projects (top 5 by total_cost DESC from getProjectBreakdown()) → /projects
     Columns: Project · Sessions · Cost
   - Top Models (top 5 by total_cost DESC from getModelBreakdown()) → /models
     Columns: Model · Messages · Cost

Changes to rootHandler (src/ui/routes.ts):
- Fetch in parallel: getOverview(), top-5 sessions, getProjectBreakdown(), getModelBreakdown()
- getDailyStats() no longer needed on front page (table removed); drop it unless retained for future sparkline use
- Pass all data to updated dashboard() template

Constraints:
- Blocked by p404-0zh, p404-vjp, p404-fky, p404-q1j (all detail pages must exist before front page links to them)
- This is the LAST task; no other task depends on it
- Spec: dashboard-detail-pages.md §Front Page Changes (authoritative over dashboard-navigation.md for table removal)
title: Front page refactor: remove tables, add quick-glance sections, link stat cards

close_reason: All Layer 1 services use Effect Context.Tag + Layer. Deprecated legacy exports removed from SourceDb, DashboardDb, IngestionService. API handlers ported to Effect. main.ts wired with DashboardDbLive.
description: Refactor all Layer 1 services to use Effect service pattern (Context.Tag + Layer). SourceDb, DashboardDb, IngestionService, StatsService. Currently services use raw bun:sqlite Database objects passed as function args. Need to wrap in Effect service machinery per coding-standard.md. Add typed error types (SourceDbError, DashboardDbError, IngestionError, StatsError) using Data.TaggedError.
title: Effect service layer: port services to Effect Context.Tag + Layer

close_reason: Implemented: endpoint now defaults to last 30 days when no params given. Both params still required if either is provided.
description: GET /api/stats/daily with no parameters returns HTTP 400: {"error":{"message":"Params start and end must be present and in YYYY-MM-DD format"}}. The endpoint has no default date range fallback. Callers must always pass ?start=YYYY-MM-DD&end=YYYY-MM-DD. This is a usability issue for the API - it should either document clearly or default to e.g. last 30 days.
title: GET /api/stats/daily returns 400 when called without query params

close_reason: Implemented safeParseJson in src/primitives/json.ts with 6 passing tests
description: Implement src/primitives/json.ts. safeParseJson(raw: string): Option.Option<unknown> - wraps JSON.parse in try/catch, returns Option.some on success, Option.none on failure. Never throws. Tests in test/primitives/json.test.ts: valid JSON, invalid JSON, empty string, nested objects, arrays, null literal.
title: primitives/json.ts: safeParseJson returning Option

close_reason: Session detail back-link now points to /sessions ('← Sessions'). Sessions list with project filter now shows '← Projects' linking to /projects. 2 tests added, all 162 pass.
description: On the session detail page (/sessions/:id), the back-link reads '← Dashboard' and links to '/'. Users arrive at session detail by clicking a row in the Sessions list, so the back-link should read '← Sessions' and link to '/sessions' (preserving any project filter if applicable). The same issue exists on the project-filtered Sessions page where '← Dashboard' would be expected to go back to /projects. The back navigation is incorrect throughout detail/sub-pages.
title: Session detail back-link points to Dashboard instead of Sessions list

close_reason: Root cause (missing CSS rules for .projects-page and .models-page) was fixed in p404-5ds (commit 4e3d921). Added regression tests in projects-page.test.ts and models-page.test.ts asserting class="projects-page" / class="models-page" is present in rendered HTML.
description: On the Projects and Models pages, the page header (containing '← Dashboard' and the h1) renders with the text flush to the left viewport edge — no left padding at all. This is because the <main> element uses class='projects-page' / class='models-page' which have no CSS rules (see p404-5ds). The session-header inside it therefore also has no container padding. Compare to Sessions page where the main uses class='sessions-list-page' which correctly gets padding: var(--gap). The h1 title and back-link on Projects/Models start at x=0.
title: Projects page header has no left padding

Users have no way to navigate between Sessions, Projects, and Models without going back to the dashboard first. A persistent nav with links to /, /sessions, /projects, /models is missing.

When ingestion runs listMessagesForSessions against the seeded dev DB, it throws SourceDbError because the message table does not exist, which surfaces as '[ingestion] error: Failed to list messages' on every sync interval during make dev.

The project name should either show a truncated hash (first 8 chars) with a tooltip for the full hash, or ideally map to a human-readable project name if available. The same hashes appear in the sessions page title when filtering by project, making the title unwieldy.

close_reason: Added .stat-card__value--sm CSS modifier and applied it to the Date Range card on the Sessions page. Font reduced from 1.75rem to 1rem, preventing the date string from wrapping.
description: On the Sessions page (/sessions), the Date Range stat card displays multi-line date ranges like '2026-03-05 → 2026-03-06' because the date string wraps inside the card. The stat-card__value has font-size: 1.75rem which is too large for a date string. All three stat cards (Total Sessions, Total Cost, Date Range) also have mismatched card heights — Total Sessions and Total Cost are tall/empty below the value while Date Range wraps and grows tall. The value font size for Date Range should be smaller, or the date format should be condensed.
title: Date Range stat card overflows on Sessions page

close_reason: Fixed: source-db SELECT now includes time_created, ingestion upsert writes it, seed script creates column and inserts value. All 152 tests pass.
description: The session detail page (/sessions/:id) shows 'CREATED: —' because timeCreated is 0 in the seed data. The formatDate function in routes.ts uses time_created from the DB which maps to timeCreated=0. When timeCreated is 0, the date should show '—' (which it does), but the real issue is the seed DB has no time_created value set. This needs investigation whether it's a data issue or the ingestion isn't populating time_created.
title: Session detail missing 'Created' timestamp (shows —)

close_reason: Fixed: changed .table thead top from 0 to var(--nav-height) in styles.css
description: In styles.css the .table thead has position: sticky; top: 0. However the site-nav is also sticky at top: 0 with height var(--nav-height) = 3rem. When scrolling a long table, the sticky thead slides under the sticky nav bar. The thead top value should be var(--nav-height) so column headers remain visible below the nav when scrolling.
title: table thead sticky top offset does not account for nav height

close_reason: Introduced .dashboard-header CSS class; dashboard template updated; test added. session-header no longer used on dashboard.

Without the back-link the h1 ('platform-404') has no alignment partner and the header section wastes vertical space for its padding-block. The dashboard header should use a simpler, dedicated style that doesn't assume the back-link+title layout pattern.

close_reason: Implemented: set +e around opencode run, SESSION_EXIT captured and logged
description: opencode run is called with || true which swallows the exit code. Capture it with set +e and log the exit code per iteration to distinguish clean exits from crashes.
title: Capture and log opencode session exit code

close_reason: Added .playwright/cli.config.json with channel:chromium to override default chrome channel. Verified playwright-cli opens with bundled chromium.
description: playwright-cli (from @playwright/cli) defaults to launching the 'chrome' channel (looks for /opt/google/chrome/chrome) rather than the Playwright-bundled 'chromium' binary. This means it fails unless --browser chromium is passed explicitly. Should configure the default browser channel so playwright-cli works out of the box. Options: set PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH, configure playwright-cli default settings, or alias/wrapper.
title: playwright-cli defaults to chrome channel instead of bundled chromium

close_reason: Implemented: all log lines use key=value structured fields

close_reason: All 6 child tasks implemented and closed
description: The ralph-loop script has minimal logging that loses history between runs, swallows exit codes, and lacks timing/structured data. This epic tracks improvements to make logs more useful for debugging and monitoring agent sessions.

close_reason: Already resolved: both .beads/config.yaml and .beads/dolt/config.yaml are aligned at port 13784, bd connects successfully.
description: bd defaulted to port 3307 while the entrypoint-managed dolt server ran on 14164. Fixed by aligning both .beads/config.yaml (dolt.port) and .beads/dolt/config.yaml (listener.port) to the recommended port 13784, cleaning stale state files, and restarting the server.
title: Dolt server port mismatch prevents bd from connecting

close_reason: Copied index.ts into image and updated CMD to bun run index.ts; covered by updated test in test/docker.test.ts
description: The Dockerfile CMD is 'bun run src/main.ts'. main.ts only exports functions (boot, runMain) with no top-level invocation, so the process starts and exits 0 immediately. The actual entry point is index.ts which calls boot(). index.ts is also not copied into the image. Result: container exits with code 0 and no server ever binds to port 3000.
title: Dockerfile CMD runs src/main.ts not index.ts so server never starts

close_reason: Implemented: removed plan file logic, added bd ready check, updated PROMPT.md

Add check_no_ready_work() using 'bd ready --json'. Update PROMPT.md to use beads workflow. See session context for full plan.
title: ralph-loop: migrate from markdown plan files to beads workflow

close_reason: Implemented sessionsPage template (src/ui/templates/sessions-list.ts), sessionsListPageHandler (src/ui/routes.ts), and registered GET /sessions in router.ts. Handles page/limit/project query params, pagination controls, mini overview bar. Red/green TDD: 1 test added, all 140 tests pass.
description:
New full paginated sessions page. Blocked by CSS (p404-6c2) for .pagination styles, and data layer (p404-xfj) for project filter support.

Implementation:
- New file: src/ui/templates/sessions-list.ts
  (dashboard-detail-pages.md names it sessions-list.ts; dashboard-navigation.md names it sessions.ts — DECISION: use sessions-list.ts to avoid collision with src/api/sessions.ts and match the session-detail.ts naming convention already in codebase)
  Interfaces:
    interface SessionsPageData {
      sessions: readonly SessionSummary[]
      page: number
      total: number
      limit: number
      projectFilter: string | null
    }
  export function sessionsPage(data: SessionsPageData): string
  Layout: back link, h1 'Sessions', mini overview bar (total sessions, total cost, date range), full sessions table, pagination controls
  Table columns: Project · Title · Messages · Cost · Input Tokens · Output Tokens · Date
  Pagination: hide 'Prev' on page 1, hide 'Next' on last page (page = ceil(total/limit))
  Differences from front-page table: adds Messages column, adds pagination, no row cap
- src/ui/routes.ts: add sessionsListPageHandler
  Reads page, limit, project query params; passes project filter to DB query
- src/api/router.ts: register GET /sessions → sessionsListPageHandler

URL params: page (default 1), limit (default 50, cap 100), project (optional, filter by projectId)

Data source: GET /api/sessions?page=N&limit=N (exists) + project filter (needs p404-xfj)

Constraints:
- Blocked by p404-6c2 (CSS: .pagination) AND p404-xfj (project filter query)
- CAN run in parallel with projects page, models page, daily API + page tasks
- BLOCKS front-page refactor (p404-00o): front page 'Recent Sessions' section links here
- Spec: dashboard-detail-pages.md §GET /sessions (authoritative on filename)
title: UI page: GET /sessions (paginated full list)

close_reason: Created CHANGELOG.md with v0.1.0 entry following Keep a Changelog format
description:
Create a CHANGELOG.md in the project root following Keep a Changelog format (https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/). Tag all work done to date as version 0.1.0.

File: CHANGELOG.md (new, project root)
Format: Keep a Changelog / Semantic Versioning

## [0.1.0] - 2026-03-04

### Added
- Project scaffolding: bun project init, TypeScript strict mode, path aliases (@/), biome formatter/linter, lefthook pre-commit hooks (format:check, lint, typecheck, test)
- Architecture: Effect-based service layer (DashboardDb, StatsService, IngestionService, SourceDb)
- Ingestion pipeline: reads opencode SQLite source DB, ingests sessions/messages/parts into local dashboard DB, periodic background sync
- API routes: GET /api/health, GET /api/stats/overview, GET /api/stats/daily, GET /api/stats/models, GET /api/stats/projects, GET /api/sessions, GET /api/sessions/:id
- UI: server-rendered HTML dashboard (dark theme, CSS design tokens), session detail page, static CSS handler
- Docker: Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml for containerised deployment
- Specs: architecture, coding standards, testing standards, git conventions, dashboard navigation, dashboard detail pages

### Fixed
- OPENCODE_DB_PATH corrected to match devenv-data volume structure
- Docker entrypoint changed from src/main.ts to index.ts
- tsconfig.json copied into Docker image for @/ path alias resolution
- Dev seed script: add missing message and part tables

Constraints:
- No code changes required — this is a docs-only task
- Should be done before the dashboard detail pages epic (p404-qow) ships, so v0.2.0 can cover that release
- No dependency on any open issue
title: Create CHANGELOG.md with v0.1.0 entry

Enhance existing GET /api/stats/overview to return real totals (total_cost, total_tokens_*, message_count) not just totalSessions. All use Effect HttpRouter, StatsService, JSON envelope. Tests in test/api/stats.test.ts.

description:
New daily drill-down page. Blocked by CSS (p404-6c2) AND API endpoint (p404-x2i).

Implementation:
- New file: src/ui/templates/daily-detail.ts
  interface DailyDetailData {
    date: string          // YYYY-MM-DD
    stat: DailyStat | null  // null if no daily_stats row for this date
    sessions: readonly SessionSummary[]
  }
  export function dailyDetailPage(data: DailyDetailData): string
  Layout: back link, h1 = date string, overview cards (Sessions · Messages · Cost · Total Tokens) — omit entirely if stat is null, sessions table sorted time_created ASC
  Error/empty state: if sessions is empty, show 'No sessions on this date.' message
  Table columns: Project · Title · Messages · Cost · Input Tokens · Output Tokens
- src/ui/routes.ts: add dailyDetailPageHandler
  Validates :date regex; returns 400 on invalid format
  Fetches GET /api/daily/:date which returns { date, stat, sessions }
- src/api/router.ts: register GET /daily/:date → dailyDetailPageHandler

Constraints:
- Blocked by p404-6c2 (CSS: .stat-card links) AND p404-x2i (API endpoint)
- CAN run in parallel with projects, models, sessions page tasks
- BLOCKS front-page refactor (p404-{front-page}): front page daily breakdown dates link here
- Spec: dashboard-navigation.md §/daily/:date Page
         dashboard-detail-pages.md §GET /daily/:date
title: UI page: GET /daily/:date

close_reason: Fixed: set PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=/opt/pw-browsers in .devenv/Dockerfile so chromium bakes into the image layer, unaffected by the ~/.cache volume mount. Collapsed apt cleanup into same RUN layer.
description: Chromium segfaulted because the binary at ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ was truncated (220MB vs expected 270MB). Root cause: ~/.cache is a volume mount that shadows files written during docker build, so the build-time browser install was invisible at runtime. A prior interrupted download left a corrupted binary on the volume. Fix: set PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=/opt/pw-browsers so chromium installs to a non-volume path baked into the image layer. Also collapsed apt-get update and rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* into proper RUN layers.
title: Chromium segfaults in devenv: system deps missing from playwright install

close_reason: All work fully implemented and committed: CSS additions, 4 new UI pages (/sessions, /projects, /models, /daily/:date), new API endpoint (/api/daily/:date), front page refactor with linked cards and quick-glance sections. 148 tests pass, validate clean.
description: Implement all new detail pages (/projects, /models, /daily/:date, /sessions) and front page refactor as specified in specs/dashboard-navigation.md and specs/dashboard-detail-pages.md. This epic covers: CSS additions, new API endpoint, new UI templates, new route handlers, front page refactor. Constraints: CSS and data layer work can start immediately and in parallel. New page templates depend on CSS being ready. Front page refactor is the final piece and depends on all quick-glance sections being testable.
title: Dashboard Detail Pages

close_reason: Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml created per architecture.md spec with tests
description: Create Docker packaging per architecture.md spec. Dockerfile: FROM oven/bun:1-slim, WORKDIR /app, COPY package.json bun.lock, RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile --production, COPY src/, EXPOSE 3000, CMD [bun run src/main.ts]. docker-compose.yml: two volumes (devenv-data read-only for opencode.db, platform-404-data at /data for dashboard.db), env vars (OPENCODE_DB_PATH, DASHBOARD_DB_PATH, PORT, SYNC_INTERVAL_MS). Currently no Dockerfile or docker-compose exists.
title: Docker: Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, volume config

description: Refactor src/services/source-db.ts to Effect service pattern. SourceDb class extends Context.Tag. Interface: listSessionsUpdatedSince(sinceMs: number): Effect<SessionRow[], SourceDbError>, listProjectsByIds(ids: string[]): Effect<ProjectRow[], SourceDbError>, listMessagesForSessions(sessionIds: string[]): Effect<MessageRow[], SourceDbError>, listPartsForMessages(messageIds: string[]): Effect<PartRow[], SourceDbError>. SourceDbLive Layer using @effect/sql-sqlite-bun, readonly:true, PRAGMA query_only=ON. SourceDbError typed error. Integration tests in test/services/source-db.test.ts using a temp sqlite file.
title: services/source-db: Effect service wrapping read-only opencode.db

close_reason: Implemented catch-all 404 route: createRouter now returns App.Default with RouteNotFound handled via Effect.catchTag, returning styled HTML 404 page.
description: Any route not registered in the router (e.g. GET /nonexistent, GET /favicon.ico) returns HTTP 404 with a completely empty body and no Content-Type header. The browser gets no HTML to render, causing ERR_ABORTED / blank page. There should be a catch-all 404 route that returns a styled HTML error page.
title: Unmatched routes return empty 404 body (no HTML)

close_reason: Implemented: REMAINING_READY snapshot from bd ready --json logged at exit
description: At exit, snapshot bd ready --json and log the count of remaining unblocked issues so it's clear how much work was left when the loop stopped.
title: Log remaining ready issue count at exit

close_reason: Clamped page to [1, lastPage] in sessionsListPageHandler; added regression test
description: GET /sessions?page=999 returns HTTP 200 with an empty table ('No sessions.') but still shows pagination as '999 / 1' and renders a 'Prev' link to page 998. Users can keep clicking Prev indefinitely on non-existent pages. The server should either clamp the page to valid range or redirect to the last valid page.
title: Pagination allows navigating to out-of-bounds pages

close_reason: Fixed OPENCODE_DB_PATH in docker-compose.yml from /opencode/opencode.db to /opencode/opencode/opencode.db to match actual volume structure
description: devenv-data volume contains opencode.db at /opencode/opencode.db inside the volume root. docker-compose.yml mounts devenv-data at /opencode, making the DB available at /opencode/opencode/opencode.db inside the container. But OPENCODE_DB_PATH is set to /opencode/opencode.db. Fix: change OPENCODE_DB_PATH to /opencode/opencode/opencode.db.
title: OPENCODE_DB_PATH in docker-compose.yml points to wrong path

title: Session detail PROJECT field is always blank

description:
Add new API endpoint for daily session drill-down. Blocked by data layer task (p404-xfj) — needs getSessionsForDate.

Implementation:
- File: src/api/sessions.ts — add apiDailyDetailHandler
- File: src/api/router.ts — register GET /api/daily/:date
- Validate :date against /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ regex; return 400 plain-text on failure
- On valid date with no sessions: return { data: { date, sessions: [] } } (NOT 404)
- Query both daily_stats (for day summary) and sessions in parallel via Promise.all / Effect.all
- Response shape: { data: { date: string, stat: DailyStat | null, sessions: SessionSummary[] } }

Constraints:
- BLOCKS p404-{daily-page}: the /daily/:date UI page fetches this endpoint
- Can be developed and tested independently of CSS
- Spec: dashboard-navigation.md §/daily/:date > New API endpoint
         dashboard-detail-pages.md §GET /daily/:date
title: API: GET /api/daily/:date endpoint

close_reason: Implemented getSessionsForDate and getSessions(projectId?) in StatsService. Both methods tested with red/green TDD. All 129 tests pass.
description:
Two data-layer additions required before /daily/:date and /sessions?project= can work.

1. Add getSessionsForDate(date: string): Effect<SessionSummary[], StatsError> to StatsService (src/services/stats.ts).
   SQL: SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE date(time_created / 1000, 'unixepoch') = ? ORDER BY time_created ASC
   Must return SessionSummary[] (same shape as existing sessions list items).

2. Add project_id filter branch to the existing sessions list query in src/services/stats.ts (or wherever getSessions is implemented).
   When project param is provided: append WHERE project_id = ? to the query.
   This unlocks /sessions?project=<projectId> filtering.

Constraints:
- getSessionsForDate blocks p404-{daily-api} (GET /api/daily/:date endpoint)
- project filter blocks p404-{sessions-page} (GET /sessions page with project filter param)
- Both can be implemented in parallel within this task.
- No changes to src/services/, src/primitives/, or src/main.ts outside of stats.ts per spec.
title: Data layer: getSessionsForDate + project filter on sessions list

close_reason: Added tsconfig.json to COPY instruction in Dockerfile; covered by new test in test/docker.test.ts
description: Dockerfile copies only package.json, bun.lock, and src/. The @/* path alias (mapped to src/* in tsconfig.json paths) is not resolvable at runtime in the container because tsconfig.json is never copied in. Bun uses tsconfig.json to resolve path aliases at runtime. Result: 'Cannot find module @/api/router from /app/src/main.ts' on container start.
title: Dockerfile missing tsconfig.json copy breaks @/ path alias in container

close_reason: Implemented step-finish part ingestion: parts are now fetched and decoded after messages, session aggregates prefer step-finish totals over message-level data. All tests pass.
description: Extend ingestion to cover full data model from architecture.md. Currently only sessions (id, project_id, title, time_updated) are ingested. Missing: (1) message ingestion with role/provider/model/cost/tokens extracted from data JSON column, (2) part ingestion for step-finish token aggregation, (3) session aggregate columns (message_count, total_cost, total_tokens_*), (4) messages table in dashboard.db, (5) daily_stats table with precomputed aggregates, (6) opencode_message ingestion cursor.
title: Ingestion: messages, parts, token/cost data, daily_stats
